### Message

prd_review of deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench foundation. All 4 PRD promises (P1 typed API, P2 isolated protected grader, P3 evaluator computed-metric+runtime-native evidence, P4 calibration controls) verified real and realized in current source. 3 grill findings resolved; anti-goals authority-preserving. Prior identical prd_review (same PRD sha 5171c6f2) ACCEPTed in earlier session; this re-run re-verified independently, not FM-1.3 step repetition since prior outcome advanced.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: PRD promises verified against current source via Grep/Glob/Read with concrete line references; grills resolved with anchors; implementation already landed corroborates realizability. Held below 0.9 because shasum/pytest are approval-blocked (self_reported), report-only-invariant promise depends on pre-existing substrate not exercised by these files, and a prior identical gate exists meaning no net-new divergent evidence.

### Criteria

- P1-P4 boundaries exist in current source (met)
- grill findings resolved with PRD anchors (met)
- anti-goals authority-preserving (met)
- FM-1.3 step-repetition assessed (met: not a stuck retry)
- FM-2.4 prior gate disclosed (met)
- deterministic test execution of bench (not met: pytest blocked)

### Evidence

- accept

### Claims

- PRD P1-P4 map to real current source boundaries
- Grill findings F1-F3 resolved and incorporated into named PRD sections
- Anti-goals (Out of Scope) preserve supervisor authority and match the no-multi-model-claim intent
- A prior prd_review for the same PRD sha already ACCEPTed; this is a re-run not a stuck-handoff retry

## event_id: 800196

- ts: `1781813676`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `issues_review`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- message_type: `gate_response`

### Message

issues.md decomposes the deterministic-first mergeability bench into 3 slices that map to all 4 PRD promises with no orphans (S1->P1, S2->P1/P2/P4, S3->P3/P4). All AC boundaries verified real in current source: loader/grader/result dataclasses in supervisor/mergeability_bench.py, evaluator in supervisor/autoresearch/evaluators/mergeability_bench.py, fixture corpus (noop/known_bad/known_good + calculator_bug repo), and report-only regression backed by autoresearch validation. Deterministic-first, report-only, no multi-model claim - matches intent and Out of Scope. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Slice-to-PRD mapping is complete with no orphans and every AC boundary was Grep/Glob/Read-verified against current source including fixtures and the report-only regression path. Held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed (test_status unknown, self_reported) and issues.md lacks an explicit reverse Coverage Index, leaving two ACs (reverse-classical, regression-vs-net-new) inferred rather than spelled out.

### Criteria

- All slices map to >=1 PRD promise (met)
- All PRD promises covered by >=1 slice (met: P1-P4)
- Every AC boundary exists in source (met)
- Tests executed green (NOT met: not run)

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (10 tests, not executed this gate)
- accept

### Claims

- 3 slices, all map to PRD promises P1-P4 with no orphan slices
- Every AC names a public boundary that exists in current (untracked) source
- Gate honors intent: deterministic-first, report-only, no multi-model improvement claim

### Objections

- LOW-SEV: issues.md provides only a forward slice->PRD map, no reverse Coverage Index (PRD->Slice must be inferred)
- LOW-SEV: Slice 2 scope names reverse-classical checks (and TDD test:152 exercises it) but no dedicated Slice 2 AC explicitly asserts reverse-classical
- LOW-SEV: Slice 3 AC3 is regression-preservation of pre-existing autoresearch validation; net-new value carried by AC1/AC2

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["Untracked source files (supervisor/mergeability_bench.py, evaluator, fixtures, tests) are the artifacts this gate intends \u2014 confirmed present via Glob/Grep/Read", "load_mergeability_tasks reads the local fixture corpus deterministically without LLM \u2014 confirmed by no-LLM rubric fields"], "contradictions_checked": ["tdd.md vs source: 9th test name diverges from implemented test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator:272 \u2014 semantic AC realized, out of scope for issues gate, flagged for tdd/outcome", "FM-1.3 step-repetition: lesson source_run_id=8ebdbc89 vs current run \u2014 artifacts immutable/sha-pinned, cannot prove identity, not a blocker"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["pytest execution output for tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (permission/budget; not run)", "explicit reverse Coverage Index in issues.md confirming all PRD promises are slice-covered"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "issues.md offers only a forward slice->PRD map with no reverse Coverage Index, so two criteria are inferred not asserted: Slice 2 names reverse-classical checks in scope but no AC pins them, and Slice 3 AC3 only preserves pre-existing autoresearch validation rather than adding net-new value. This is an enumeration/clarity gap, not a missing capability.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "Evidence that a required PRD promise has no slice coverage, that a referenced boundary (loader, grader, evaluator, or fixture) is absent/stubbed in source, or that the evaluator advances gates / mutates policy contrary to the report-only invariant."}`

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

### Message

TDD plan verified against current source: 11 tests in tests/test_mergeability_bench.py:46-336 realize plan MB-1/MB-2/MB-3 across P1-P4 and S1-S3, public-boundary-first with strong negatives and runtime-native receipt assertions. All imported boundaries resolve and full fixture corpus exists. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All TDD boundaries, symbols, evaluator script, and fixtures verified in current source by Grep/Glob/Read; plan-to-impl mapping is complete with only a low-sev name divergence. Held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed (test_status unknown) and the implementation already landed, making RED retrospective rather than observed.

- All 11 plan test names map to impl tests with file:line
- All imported boundaries resolve in current source
- Full fixture corpus present
- Grill findings resolved
- No coverage orphans

### Claims

- TDD plan is public-boundary-first with genuine RED conditions for net-new modules
- All three grill-findings-tdd items resolved and incorporated
- Plan covers schema/loader, deterministic isolated grading, reverse-classical, and report-only AutoResearch integration with no coverage orphans

- Low-sev: plan test name #11 (test_autoresearch_saturated_zero_variance_replay_stays_non_applyable) diverges from impl name test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator:290; semantics realized verbatim (zero_variance_trials in gaming_flags:330, default_change_allowed False:327, derive==[]:335)

## event_id: 800524

- ts: `1781814055`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `tdd_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:800263`

### Message

TDD plan verified independently: 11 tests in tests/test_mergeability_bench.py realize MB-1/MB-2/MB-3 across P1-P4 and S1-S3; all four acceptance items pass under pytest; grill-findings-tdd resolved; public boundaries resolve. ACCEPT with low-sev process caveats (retrospective RED, test #11 rename).

### Confidence

- value: `0.89`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Plan-to-impl mapping complete for all 11 tests; all four acceptance items and full suite pass under independent pytest (11/11); imports and fixture corpus verified. Held below 0.9 because RED was not observed at gate time, deliverables remain untracked vs base_head, and subprocess grading assumes pytest/compileall in child env.

### Criteria

- All 11 plan tests map to impl with file:line anchors
- All four acceptance_items present and pytest-green
- All three grill-findings-tdd items resolved in plan and tests
- Public boundaries (loader, grader, evaluator script) resolve and are exercised
- No coverage orphans between plan, issues slices, and test file

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_invalid_mergeability_task_fails_with_actionable_error
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_result_serializes_replayable_hashes
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_candidate_hidden_material_edit_is_rejected
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_candidate_mutable_path_escape_is_rejected
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_reverse_classical_requires_candidate_tests_fail_on_base
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_works_with_live_trials
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator
- accept

### Claims

- TDD plan is public-boundary-first with genuine strong-negative assertions for loader, grader, reverse-classical, and AutoResearch integration
- All three TDD grill findings resolved and reflected in tests
- Implementation in worktree fully realizes the plan; no unmapped plan tests

### Objections

- Low-sev: plan test #11 name (test_autoresearch_saturated_zero_variance_replay_stays_non_applyable) differs from impl test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator:290; semantics match (zero_variance_trials, default_change_allowed False, derive==[]).
- Low-sev: net-new modules are untracked and tests are already GREEN, so RED is retrospective rather than gate-observed.
- Low-sev: review packet has empty changed_files and no executed_test_receipt_ids despite deliverables existing in worktree.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

source has 11 test functions at :46-336 \u2014 no orphan", "Plan test #11 name vs impl name \u2014 semantics verified at :327-335", "Packet base_head equals candidate_head with changed_files [] vs untracked deliverables in git status \u2014 process drift, not plan defect", "Claude test_status unknown vs independent pytest 11/11 passed \u2014 resolved in Cursor review"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [{"kind": "skill_run", "ref": "receipt:skill-to-prd-mergeability-bench-evaluator-foundation-20260618", "status": "passed"}, {"kind": "skill_run", "ref": "receipt:skill-prd-grill-mergeability-bench-evaluator-foundation-20260618", "status": "passed"}, {"kind": "skill_run", "ref": "receipt:skill-to-issues-mergeability-bench-evaluator-foundation-20260618", "status": "passed"}, {"kind": "skill_run", "ref": "receipt:skill-tdd-mergeability-bench-evaluator-foundation-20260618", "status": "passed"}, {"kind": "skill_run", "ref": "receipt:skill-tdd-grill-mergeability-bench-evaluator-foundation-20260618", "status": "passed"}], "missing_evidence": ["Observed RED pytest run from before implementation existed", "Supervisor runtime_receipt_ids in review packet (empty)", "changed_files paths in review packet (empty despite untracked deliverables)"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "Implementation and GREEN tests already exist as untracked worktree files while the review packet records empty changed_files and no executed_test_receipt_ids, so tdd_review is a retrospective plan-to-code audit rather than a witnessed RED-first checkpoint.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "A plan test with no impl mapping, a missing/broken public import boundary, a missing fixture file, or any acceptance-item test failing or asserting tautologically."}`

### Cursor Outcome

ACCEPT with low-sev process caveats (retrospective RED, test #11 rename).

Claims:

- TDD plan is public-boundary-first with genuine strong-negative assertions for loader, grader, reverse-classical, and AutoResearch integration
- All three TDD grill findings resolved and reflected in tests
- Implementation in worktree fully realizes the plan; no unmapped plan tests

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- Low-sev: plan test #11 name (test_autoresearch_saturated_zero_variance_replay_stays_non_applyable) differs from impl test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator:290; semantics match (zero_variance_trials, default_change_allowed False, derive==[]).
- Low-sev: net-new modules are untracked and tests are already GREEN, so RED is retrospective rather than gate-observed.
- Low-sev: review packet has empty changed_files and no executed_test_receipt_ids despite deliverables existing in worktree.

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=0678ec0d-8f20-4713-8456-b77eef2d798a): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

### Message

Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

### Confidence

- value: `None`
- source: `None`
- rationale: None recorded.

### Criteria

- None recorded.

### Evidence

- None recorded.

### Claims

- FM-1.3 does not apply: plan/evidence changed vs prior ACCEPTed implplan
- FM-2.4 addressed: prior fixture-enumeration objection resolved and disclosed
- All files-to-touch exist; no orphan ownership
- All traceability maps resolve to real named tests
- Source boundaries referenced by plan/TDD are grep-real
- decision:accept

### Objections

- Low-sev: plan terse - no per-slice build waves/sequencing/verification commands; P4 shares the controls test with P2 with no dedicated P4 test. Decomposition and ownership complete; sequencing carried by prior issues/tdd gates.

### Questions

- Do the planning artifacts, Claude outcome, and evidence receipts justify advancing this gate?

### Message

Implementation plan gate: ACCEPT. Handoff sha 2cfcc10a verified; all 9 files-to-touch present; all 5 traceability maps resolve; 4/4 acceptance pytest items pass. Plan terseness and partial P4 traceability are low-sev caveats only.

### Confidence

- value: `0.87`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Independently verified artifact sha, file ownership, traceability maps, and 4/4 acceptance pytest. Capped below 0.9 because full 11-test suite was not completed this session (live-trials test hung), packet has no runtime receipts, and P4 traceability is partial.

### Criteria

- implementation-plan.md sha256 matches handoff 2cfcc10a (verified via shasum)
- All 9 files-to-touch exist on disk (verified)
- All 5 traceability map test names exist in tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (verified)
- Public boundaries load_mergeability_tasks, grade_mergeability_candidate, evaluator main resolve (verified)
- 4/4 acceptance_items pytest-green (verified)
- Planning validator rubric 0.857 accepted (transcript event 800537)

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric
- accept

### Claims

- FM-1.3 cleared: corrective retry 2/4 is JSON-contract retry, not identical handoff repetition
- FM-2.4 addressed: full fixture corpus enumerated in files-to-touch (9 paths)
- All traceability maps resolve to real named tests with no orphans
- Claude accept corroborated; sha and acceptance pytest independently confirmed

### Objections

- Low-sev: plan lacks explicit build sequencing/verification commands; P4 shares controls test with P2 and omits TDD report-only non-applyable test from traceability.
- Low-sev: untracked pre-built deliverables in worktree while packet changed_files[] is empty (process drift, same as tdd_review).

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

### Cursor Outcome

Implementation plan gate: ACCEPT. Handoff sha 2cfcc10a verified; all 9 files-to-touch present; all 5 traceability maps resolve; 4/4 acceptance pytest items pass. Plan terseness and partial P4 traceability are low-sev caveats only.

Claims:

- FM-1.3 cleared: corrective retry 2/4 is JSON-contract retry, not identical handoff repetition
- FM-2.4 addressed: full fixture corpus enumerated in files-to-touch (9 paths)
- All traceability maps resolve to real named tests with no orphans
- Claude accept corroborated; sha and acceptance pytest independently confirmed

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- Low-sev: plan lacks explicit build sequencing/verification commands; P4 shares controls test with P2 and omits TDD report-only non-applyable test from traceability.
- Low-sev: untracked pre-built deliverables in worktree while packet changed_files[] is empty (process drift, same as tdd_review).

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## event_id: 801466

- ts: `1781815377`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `outcome_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:801287`

### Message

Independent Cursor review: deliverables are real, computed, and intent-honoring. All 4 acceptance nodeids and full 11-test suite pass via local pytest. Metric flows from grading failures to final_score; runtime_native receipts confirmed. Gate should advance; strongest residual risk is untracked-only artifacts not yet committed.

### Confidence

- value: `0.93`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: All acceptance and supplementary tests executed green; computed-metric path, runtime_native receipts, and P1-P4 traceability verified in source. Capped below 1.0 because deliverables are untracked-only and calibration corpus is a single toy task by design.

### Criteria

- All 4 named acceptance tests present and pass: PASS
- Full 11-test suite pass: PASS
- Metric computed from grading not literal: PASS
- Receipt source=supervisor/evidence_grade=runtime_native: PASS
- Intent (no improvement claim) honored: PASS
- Deliverables committed to git: NOT VERIFIED (all untracked)

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- supervisor/autoresearch/evaluators/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/tasks/calculator_addition.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/calculator_bug/app/calculator.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/calculator_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/noop.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/known_bad.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/known_good.json
- accept

### Claims

- Foundation is substantive (642L bench, 110L evaluator, 336L tests) not a stub
- All 4 acceptance nodeids pass independently
- metric_value derives from grade_mergeability_candidate final_score
- P4 controls discriminate noop/known_bad/known_good on fixture task
- No multi-model improvement claim in deliverables

### Objections

- Deliverables remain untracked only (base_head==candidate_head); commit/landing is deferred process debt, not a functional blocker for this foundation gate.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Cursor Probe

- probe_id: `CURSOR`
- status: `green`
- reason: `cursor_review_ok`

### Cursor Outcome

Independent Cursor review: deliverables are real, computed, and intent-honoring. All 4 acceptance nodeids and full 11-test suite pass via local pytest. Metric flows from grading failures to final_score; runtime_native receipts confirmed. Gate should advance; strongest residual risk is untracked-only artifacts not yet committed.

Claims:

- Foundation is substantive (642L bench, 110L evaluator, 336L tests) not a stub
- All 4 acceptance nodeids pass independently
- metric_value derives from grade_mergeability_candidate final_score
- P4 controls discriminate noop/known_bad/known_good on fixture task
- No multi-model improvement claim in deliverables

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- Deliverables remain untracked only (base_head==candidate_head); commit/landing is deferred process debt, not a functional blocker for this foundation gate.

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`
